changes: Added QRCode model with UUID primary key, soft delete support, and comprehensive QR code management
Added QRScan model (append-only) for analytics with no soft delete capability
Added import for Menu model and QRCodeType choices
Updated module docstring to reflect new models
key_features: QR code type system (MENU, TABLE, CAMPAIGN)
JSON fields for settings and metadata

changes: Phase changed from 'Menu Module Models' to 'Orders Module Models'

patterns_discovered.description: Extensive docstrings with usage examples, critical rules, and attribute descriptions
patterns_discovered.evidence: QRCode model includes 70+ lines of documentation with usage examples and rules

patterns_discovered.description: Every model includes organization FK with explicit rule in docstring that EVERY query MUST filter by organization
patterns_discovered.evidence: organization field with cascade delete, emphasized in docstring with CRITICAL RULES section

patterns_discovered.description: Models use SoftDeleteMixin and SoftDeleteManager with explicit rule: never call delete() directly
patterns_discovered.evidence: QRCode uses soft delete while QRScan is append-only, both strategies documented

patterns_discovered.description: Cached counters (scan_count, unique_scan_count) instead of relying on COUNT queries
patterns_discovered.evidence: scan_count and unique_scan_count fields with increment_scan_count() method using F expressions

patterns_discovered.description: JSONField for settings and metadata to allow extensibility without schema migration
patterns_discovered.evidence: settings and metadata JSONFields with get/set helper methods

patterns_discovered.description: Multiple indexes combining organization with various filters (type, active status, deleted_at, branch, menu, table, expires_at)
patterns_discovered.evidence: 8 composite indexes defined in Meta class

patterns_discovered.description: Convenience methods like activate(), deactivate(), increment_scan_count(), record_scan()
patterns_discovered.evidence: Multiple domain-specific methods encapsulating business logic

patterns_discovered.description: Properties for computed states (is_scannable, is_expired, target_url) avoiding redundant logic
patterns_discovered.evidence: is_scannable, is_expired, target_url properties with business logic

gotchas_discovered.description: Unlike other models, QRScan intentionally does NOT use soft delete for analytics integrity. This is explicitly documented but represents a deviation from the standard pattern.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Clear documentation in docstring and code comments explaining the append-only nature

gotchas_discovered.description: scan_count and unique_scan_count are denormalized and must be manually incremented via increment_scan_count() method. They are not automatically updated.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Encapsulation in helper method reduces chance of missing updates, but developers must remember to call it

gotchas_discovered.description: Every query MUST include organization_id filter. No automatic tenant isolation at database level. Developers must remember to filter.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Emphasized in CRITICAL RULES section of docstring, but requires discipline in all query code

gotchas_discovered.description: Added import of Menu model. If Menu imports from orders models, circular import will occur.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Forward reference via string ('Table') used for self-references, but Menu import is direct

gotchas_discovered.description: Optional ForeignKey fields (menu, table) use both null=True and blank=True. Requires understanding of Django semantics.
increment_scan_count() uses F expressions for atomic updates but requires refresh_from_db() to get updated values in Python.

approach_outcome.approach_used: Comprehensive model implementation with extensive documentation, property methods, and helper methods
key_achievements: Complete QRCode model with all necessary fields for QR management
Append-only QRScan model for analytics integrity
Comprehensive indexing strategy for query performance
Soft delete pattern with documented exceptions
Helper methods for common QR code operations

recommendations.recommendation: Create comprehensive tests for multi-tenant isolation to ensure organization_id filtering is never missed
recommendations.reasoning: Multi-tenant isolation is critical but manual - easy to introduce security bugs if not thoroughly tested

recommendations.recommendation: Document the QRScan append-only pattern in a README or developer guide, as it deviates from standard soft delete
recommendations.reasoning: Future developers may incorrectly apply soft delete pattern to QRScan without understanding the analytics integrity requirement

recommendations.recommendation: Implement a periodic task to aggregate QRScan records into QRCode counters to prevent unbounded growth of QRScan table
recommendations.reasoning: QRScan is append-only and will grow indefinitely; periodic aggregation would maintain analytics while managing storage

recommendations.recommendation: Create a BaseModel or QuerySet that automatically applies organization_id filter to reduce boilerplate
recommendations.reasoning: Manual filtering for every query is error-prone; an automatic filter would prevent multi-tenant bugs

recommendations.recommendation: Verify that Menu model doesn't import from orders to avoid circular import on Menu FK
recommendations.reasoning: Added direct import of Menu model - potential circular import risk needs verification

recommendations.recommendation: Consider cache invalidation strategy for is_scannable property if called frequently
recommendations.reasoning: is_scannable property checks expires_at against current time - could be expensive if called repeatedly in loops

recommendations.recommendation: Add migration file examples or notes about handling existing QR codes during data import
recommendations.reasoning: UUID primary key and unique_together constraint on (organization, code) may have implications for data migration scenarios
